name: Loudoun County Public Schools
about: Loudoun County Public Schools serves approximately 84,000 students in Loudoun County, Virginia — one of the wealthiest and fastest-growing counties in the United States, located in the outer Washington D.C. suburbs.
calendarNotes: Loudoun County Public Schools serves one of the fastest-growing and highest-income counties in the United States, driven by the data center and technology corridor along the Dulles Technology Corridor. The school year starts in mid-August. Spring Break falls in late March, aligned with the broader Northern Virginia region. Loudoun's rapid growth means new schools open regularly and attendance boundaries change frequently — families in recently developed neighborhoods should verify their assigned school directly with the district. The area has a large number of tech company employees who often have flexible remote work schedules, making precise Spring Break dates especially important for coordinating family travel plans.
